title: Mandalorian Crusaders

{{Quote|The Mandalore clans are the best fighters in the galaxy... we don't need to wait for a few Jedi.|[[Mandalore the Indomitable]]|<ref name="TOTJTSW1" />}}
The '''Mandalorian Crusaders''' were the first documented type of [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]], established by [[Mandalore the First]], sometime after [[7000 BBY]] and under the command of [[Mandalore the Conqueror]], later [[Mandalore the Indomitable]], they eventually became involved in the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]] and fought for the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]]-turned-[[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]]. The Crusaders were traditional Mandalorian warriors,<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide" /> placing highest value on gaining honor and glory in combat.

==History==
===Origins===
{{Quote|For my people it's the honor and glory of battle that rules us. It's through combat that we prove our worth, gain renown and make our fortunes.|[[Canderous Ordo]]|<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]''</ref>}}
According to legend, the Crusaders were first formed on the planet Mandalore around the year [[7000 BBY]] by [[Mandalore the First]], who is said to have conquered the world by defeating the [[Mythosaur/Legends|Mythosaur]], the great beasts who dominated the planet. After conquering [[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore]], the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]] ventured on [[Mandalorian Crusades|crusades]] in the years leading up to the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]], gaining them valuable planets such as [[Ordo/Legends|Ordo]], [[Concord Dawn/Legends|Concord Dawn]], [[Shogun (planet)|Shogun]] and [[Gargon/Legends|Gargon]].<ref name="The Essential Atlas" />

===The Great Sith War===
[[File:Battle_of_Kuar.jpg|thumb|left|200px|[[Mandalore the Indomitable]] is defeated by [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]].]]
The Crusaders roamed space seeking war, making conquered worlds their home.<ref name="SWInsider" /> Led by the warrior [[Mandalore the Indomitable]], they arrived in the [[Empress Teta/Legends|Empress Teta]] system by [[3997 BBY]]. Riding their Basilisk War Droids, they destroyed a [[Krath/Legends|Krath]] [[Tetan carbonite smelter]] in orbit to provoke the Krath into fighting them. Ulic Qel-Droma, a Sith Lord associated with the Krath agreed to duel [[Mandalore the Indomitable|Mandalore]] on Mandalore's newly conquered world of [[Kuar]].<ref name="TOTJTSW1" />

Ulic became the victor, and the [[Brotherhood of the Sith]] gained command of Mandalore's forces, an act that left many Mandalorians frustrated. A few extremists amongst the clans even declared themselves leaderless, as Mandalore in effect lost his title to Ulic, and Qel-Droma being an outsider couldn't take it. Too few in numbers, the voices of those objectors wasn't heard. These same objectors later founded the [[Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders/Legends|Neo-Crusader]] movement on Mandalore.<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide" />

The Crusaders fought on Ulic's side in the [[Battle of Foerost (Great Sith War)|Battle of Foerost]], [[Battle of Coruscant (Great Sith War)|Coruscant]] and eventually were ordered to [[Battle of Onderon (Great Sith War)|Onderon]] by Exar Kun. During that battle, they survived only by retreating to the [[Dxun/Legends|Dxun moon]]. Mandalore himself was killed by the beasts of Dxun. The defeat left the Mandalorians scattered, many feeling betrayed and speaking of vengeance against the Republic and its Jedi guardians.<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide" />

===The Mandalorian Wars===
[[File:RohlanDyre.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Rohlan Dyre, a veteran Mandalorian Crusader who questioned their motives]]
Soon after the Sith War, a [[Mandalore the Ultimate|new Mandalore]] named the Ultimate rose to power. He called upon aides to help him reshape the identity of the Mandalorian forces, and as a group of warriors named the Neo-Crusaders began calling for the reestablishment of the tradition, Mandalore used the group as a foundation for his goal. During the onslaught on the outer rim, a veteran Crusader named [[Rohlan Dyre]] began to question the motive behind the war and specifically the Neo-Crusaders. Ironically, Rohlan soon became a tool for Mandalore, who used him as a cautionary tale promoting the Neo-Crusaders.<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic 21: Daze of Hate, Part 3">[[Knights of the Old Republic 21|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 21]]</ref>

During the invasion into the Republic, Mandalore had Neo-Crusaders present in every unit to spread the ideals of uniformity and order in an attempt to boost morale and convert Crusaders to this new way of life. A few resisted, but many more converted, such as the warrior [[Canderous Ordo]]. During the [[Taris Siege]], the Crusader quickly became a shrinking minority in the Mandalorian forces. There were still some objectors, and a few even turned down commands from [[Cassus Fett]].<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic 23: Knights of Suffering, Part 2">[[Knights of the Old Republic 23|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 23]]</ref>

After the [[Battle of Malachor V|final battle]] of the Mandalorian Wars the Mandalorians again scattered across the galaxy. Many honorable warriors continued the traditions<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide" /> while many others swallowed their pride and became [[Mercenary/Legends|soldiers for hire]].<ref name="SWInsider" />

===Legacy===
[[File:AlmecReinstatementSpeech-SoR.png|thumb|left|250px|Almec speaks to the people of Sundari behind a mural of Mandalorian Crusaders decapitating Jedi.]]
Two murals depicting Crusaders battling Jedi were located under a central balcony of the [[Sundari Royal Palace/Legends|Sundari Royal Palace]] in [[Sundari/Legends|Sundari]] on Mandalore during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. Both [[Pre Vizsla/Legends|Pre Vizsla]], after [[Takeover of Mandalore/Legends|taking over Mandalore]], and [[Almec/Legends|Almec]], after being reinstated as [[Prime Minister/Legends|Prime Minister]] by [[Darth Maul/Legends|Darth Maul]], made speeches from the balcony,<ref name="sor">{{TCW|Shades of Reason}}</ref> and [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]] dueled with Maul and [[Savage Opress/Legends|Savage Opress]] on the balcony and in front of the murals.<ref name="Lawless">{{TCW|The Lawless}}</ref>

==Doctrine and organization==
According to legend, the Mandalorian forefathers used to be deeply religious and used warfare as a ritual to their gods. The society changed over the millennia, including their once sophisticated holy laws devolved into the Canons of Honor, and war itself became the object of worship. Any campaign they waged became a Crusade if it promised holy carnage.<ref name="SWInsider" /> Traditional Crusaders had no written laws, but the few rules that existed were sacred to their society. The most important were the ''[[Resol'nare]]'', or the six actions.<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide" /> Other norms included the Canons of Honor, which helped warriors to gain honor and glory by participating in battles and being loyal to one's clan.<ref name="SWInsider" /> Unlike the Neo-Crusader movement, the Crusaders didn't conscript or seek out warriors to join their cause, but attracted individuals by the examples they set. The resol'nare had nothing to say when it came to species, and amongst the [[Taung/Legends|Taungs]] were a great variety of different species all united and dedicated to the movement as equals.<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide" />

A society of traditionally nomadic warriors, the Mandalorian Crusaders did not recognize bureaucracy. Division of labor sorted itself out organically. A warrior needed to be self-sufficient and competent, and each warrior was responsible for their own feeding and supplying. Instead of relying on helmet transceivers, Crusaders carried information through the forces by sound and vision. They always kept an eye on their fellow warriors to respond when battle situations changed. Since the Crusaders had no ranks, they traditionally followed the most experienced veteran on the field into battle.<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide" /> Even with no formal units, the Crusaders often named these informal formations. One such was [[Viper Alpha]], who fought in the [[Battle of Ossus (Great Sith War)|Battle of Ossus]] during the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Sith War]].<ref name="TOTJTSW6">[[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 6|''Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War'' 6]]</ref>

==Armor==
[[File:Crusader.jpg|right|thumb|Crusader Armor.]]
Mandalorian Crusaders wore armor categorized as either [[Mandalorian combat suit|combat suits]] or [[Mandalorian battle armor|battle armors]], with the former as the favored model while the latter of which was sometimes worn by veterans.<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide" /> Each warrior customized their suits of armor, internally and externally, to serve their preferences. As a result each suit was unique to the Mandalorian wearing it. Most suits were designed for personal combat, considered to be the most honorable form of combat. External appearance of Crusader armor was often organic, with the hard parts of the armor decorated with spikes and straps. During the Sith war, Crusaders often employed [[ROCKET|Zim Systems Rocket Packs]].<ref name="SWInsider" /> The warriors were known to ride the open-mount [[Basilisk war droid/Legends|Basilisk war droids]] in space. The Crusader armor was airtight and included enough oxygen to allow a Crusader ten hours of breathable air in the vacuum of space, likely a result of the mounts.<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide" />
